title: Bridge Essay: The role of the motor system in action imagination and comprehension.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**The role of the motor system in action imagination and comprehension.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2025). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2025.106392. PMID: 41043494. Covert actions encompass cognitive processes such as motor imagery, action observation, and action language. These processes ignite motor simulations within the mind and engage neural circuits underlying sensorimotor functions, offering promising potential for optimizing motor performance and rehabilitation strategies. Yet, despite broad consensus on the motor system's involvement, its precise role - whether a dynamic embodiment mechanism or a mere epiphenomenon - remains a matter of intense deb
